_comment: News trigger fires only if an article title/summary matches >=1 keyword AND comes from a whitelisted RSS feed below. Matching is case-insensitive substring matching on title+summary.
_comment_google_news: Google News RSS search feeds (news.google.com/rss/search?q=...) -- no API key, no account, publicly documented URL format, used the same way as any other RSS feed below. Added for AI and broader markets/finance coverage the outlet-specific feeds above don't reach on their own -- hence the added AI/finance keywords above, so articles from these topic searches can actually pass the keyword filter. Same 'broken feed logged and skipped, never a hard failure' handling as every other feed (see news_rss.py).
_comment_ai_feeds: Before adding TechCrunch/The Verge/VentureBeat, AI coverage relied only on the generic 'Google News: AI' keyword search -- these three add dedicated AI-outlet feeds (product launches, funding, research) on top of that. Same fail-safe handling: a dead/wrong feed URL is caught per-feed (news_rss.py), logged, and skipped -- never a hard failure for the whole run.
